projects.title: 3D Base Support Upgrade
projects.full_description:
I already have a proven base-support model that prints and functions well. Now I need it adapted to carry an additional component, which calls for new mounting points on a right-side extension. 

Your task is to adapt the current file, design a robust extension, and reintegrate the geometry so the whole piece remains one clean, printable body. Dimensions of the original footprint must stay untouched so it still mates with the equipment it already supports; everything you add should respect the same wall-thickness, tolerance and strength assumptions. My printer is an FDM machine, so overhangs, bridging and filament flow have to be considered. 

Deliverables 
• STL file
• Exported STL ready for slicing 
• One short PDF or screenshot set highlighting the new right-side mounting features and their key measurements 

I’ll test-fit and print the first iteration; one refinement cycle is included before we sign off. If you’re comfortable engineering functional parts for real-world loads and understand manufacturability in 3D printing, I’d love to collaborate.

projects.title: AI-Powered Secure Voice-Controlled System Development
projects.full_description:
Freelancer ya kisi bhi B2B platform par project dalne ke liye aapka description **"Professional, Technical, aur Security-focused"** hona chahiye. Jab aap developer ko clear scope dete hain, tabhi aapko best talent milta hai.
Niche diye gaye description ko aap copy-paste kar sakte hain:
### **Project Title:**
**Development of an AI-Powered Secure Voice-Controlled Multi-Account Management System**
### **Project Description:**
I am looking to build a highly secure, private AI-powered assistant capable of handling multi-account operations (Banking & Social Media) via voice commands. The system must be strictly accessible only by my voice, ensuring maximum privacy and automation.
#### **Core Requirements:**
 1. **Voice Biometrics/Speaker Recognition:** The system must integrate advanced voice authentication to ensure that it responds **only** to my specific voice, preventing any unauthorized access via recording or mimicry.
 2. **Multi-Account Integration:** Seamless and secure integration with bank accounts (via secure APIs/OAuth) and social media handles for automated tasks.
 3. **Command Execution:** The AI should process complex voice commands to execute actions like checking balances, monitoring account status, and managing social media workflows.
 4. **Security & Privacy (Crucial):**
 * End-to-end encryption for all API tokens and authentication keys.
 * No storage of passwords in plain text (use of secure vaults like HashiCorp or AWS Secrets Manager).
 * Mandatory "Secondary Confirmation" for any financial transactions.
 5. **Technical Stack:**
 * Voice Engine: High-fidelity Speech-to-Text (STT) and Natural Language Understanding (NLU).
 * Security: OAuth 2.0, Multi-Factor Authentication (MFA), and Anti-Spoofing voice detection.
 * Infrastructure: Scalable and secure deployment (Private Cloud/On-premises preference).
#### **What I Expect from the Freelancer:**
 * Proven experience in **Agentic AI** and **Voice Biometrics**.
 * Experience in integrating **Banking/Fintech APIs** with strict security compliance.
 * Prior experience in developing AI assistants with strong guardrails.
 * The code must be modular, well-documented, and subject to security audits.
#### **Deliverables:**
 * Fully functional prototype of the AI voice assistant.
 * Documentation of the security architecture.
 * Admin dashboard to monitor all connected accounts and logs.

• Thoughtful use of exotic elements—think rare spices, unexpected aromatics, or international condiments—that remain accessible to a serious home cook or small bistro kitchen. 
• Ingredient lists in both metric and imperial units, plus concise chef’s notes explaining the creative choices and possible substitutions. 
• High-level food-safety guidance specific to poultry and sous-vide techniques.

I’m open on the number of dishes as long as each one feels unique, refined and restaurant-quality. If you have previous work that showcases inventive flavour pairings or advanced sous-vide know-how, include a brief sample or link so I can see your style.

Once we agree on the outline, I’ll expect the full write-up in a single, well-formatted document ready for editorial review.

I can pay 600 INR per dish and ready to buy 3 recipes to start with. 

This is going to be ongoing work.

projects.title: Self-Hosted n8n CRM Automation: ClickUp-Whatsapp - 21/05/2026 03:02 EDT
projects.full_description:
Project Overview
We are a plastic surgery clinic operating across two locations . We use ClickUp as our CRM to manage patient leads, and Whatsapp API (powered by Voxa) as our WhatsApp and phone communication platform. We need a complete, production-ready automation system built entirely on self-hosted n8n that keeps both platforms in perfect real-time sync.
________________________________________
CRITICAL ACCEPTANCE CONDITION — READ BEFORE BIDDING
This project will not be considered complete, and no deliverables will be considered received, under any circumstances, unless the entire system is fully working and jointly verified end-to-end by both the developer and our team in a live testing session. Submission of files, workflows, or documentation alone does not constitute delivery. The project is only accepted when every flow listed in this brief has been demonstrated to work correctly in our live production environment, in real time, with real data. Any partial functionality, workarounds, or "it works on my end" claims will not be accepted. Full working system or no delivery — there is no middle ground.

________________________________________
Tech Stack
• n8n self-hosted v2.20.8 on Ubuntu 22.04 via Docker
• ClickUp workspace with two lists corresponding to each of our two locations
• Voxa WhatsApp + phone platform
• Google Forms linked to Google Sheets for lead capture
• ClickUp API Key, Whatsapp API OAuth2 credentials, and server access will be provided upon hiring
________________________________________
Deliverables
1. Whatsapp API → ClickUp (Inbound Sync)
• Every inbound WhatsApp message or phone call from an unregistered number must create a new ClickUp task in the correct location list based on the contact’s city field
• New tasks must be created with status OPEN and assigned via round-robin to one of the city coordinators based on both channels (clickup & whatsapp api) on consistent hash of phone number
• If the number already exists in ClickUp, no new task is created, instead a structured communication log comment is added to the existing task (channel, direction, time, duration, outcome, summary), 
• If the communication was two-way (client replied), the task status must automatically advance to Follow Up 1, so as with any successful tw way communication, moving the card from Follow UP x to Follow up X+1, unless the coordinator chose it not to move (we have a circle of 9 Follow ups)
• If one-way (no reply from client), the task stays at OPEN, until the coordinator chooses to change the status 
2. ClickUp → Whatsapp API (Outbound Sync)and vice versa 
• Any status change in fields or status on a ClickUp task must reflect immediately on the corresponding Whatsapp API contact's custom property and vice versa 
• Any status change in fields or status on a Whatsapp API task must reflect immediately on the corresponding clickup contact's custom property and vice versa 
• Any assignee change on ClickUp must update the assigned agent on the Whatsapp API conversation and vice versa 
• Any custom field update on ClickUp must update the matching custom property on whatsapp api and vice versa 
• All 16 ClickUp statuses must map to whatsapp api custom fields : OPEN, Follow Up 1–9, Wednesday Clinic, Saturday Clinic, Sunday Clinic, Procedure, Reserved, Build Relation, Disqualified, every change in any field on wither side should reflect on the other 
3. Follow-Up Stage Progression
• Each successful two-way communication must automatically advance the ClickUp task to the next Follow Up stage (e.g. Follow Up 3 → Follow Up 4), capped at Follow Up 9
• Each stage change must trigger a structured comment log on the ClickUp task including a communication log 
• Stage must reflect in real time on the Whatsapp API contact's properties
4. Google Form → ClickUp + Whatsapp api
• Google Form fields linked automatically sinced to a google sheet: Date of Submission (auto), Name, Phone Number, City, Service, Intended Timeline, Budget Range, linked to Google Sheets tab "Site Form"
• A Google Apps Script must POST every new submission to n8n
• n8n must check if the phone number already exists in ClickUp
• If exists: leave a tagged comment on the existing task for the assigned coordinator, and update Whatsapp API contact fields
• If new: create a ClickUp task in the correct city list, assign via round-robin on both ClickUp and whatsapp API , create a new Whatsapp API contact with all available fields populated
5. Custom Field Parity (ClickUp ↔ Hatif)
• All ClickUp custom fields must have exact matching custom properties created in Whatsapp API via POST /v1/contact-property-definitions
• Full field list to be created/mapped:
Field ClickUp Type Whatsapp API Type
Phone Phone Text
Client Name Short Text Text
Age Number Number
Weight Short Text Text
Height Number Number
BMI Number Number
Max Weight Reached Number Number
Gender Dropdown Select
Nationality Location Text
Saudi or Foreigner Dropdown Select
Residential City Location Text
Marital Status Dropdown Select
Occupation Short Text Text
Lead Medium Dropdown Select
Portal Source Dropdown Select
Services Labels Text
Stage/Status Dropdown Select
Clinic Reservation Date Date Date
Operation Date Date Date
Reason for Disqualification Dropdown Select
Chronic Diseases Labels Text
Smoker Dropdown Select
Has Stretch Marks Dropdown Select
Past Medical History Text Text
Past Operational History Text Text
Previous Pregnancies Short Text Text
Sick Leave Needed Dropdown Select
Min Cost Currency Number
Max Cost Currency Number
ClickUp Task ID Short Text Text
Follow Up Date 1–9 Date × 9 Date × 9
• Special naming rule: Whatsapp API contact Name field always mirrors ClickUp Task and vice versa 
• Name. The ClickUp field 0-Client Name maps to a separate Whatsapp API property called "Client Name"
• Any new custom field added to ClickUp in the future must automatically trigger creation of a matching property in the Whatsapp api tool
• Any field deleted from ClickUp must trigger deletion of the matching Whatsapp API property, this capability should be limited to admins
6. Initial Historical Data Migration
• A manually-triggerable n8n workflow that fetches ALL existing tasks from both ClickUp location lists
• For each task: check if a Whatsapp API contact with the same phone number already exists
• If exists: update with all mapped fields from ClickUp
• If not: create a new Whatsapp API contact with all fields populated
• Must process in batches to avoid rate limits
• Must produce a reconciliation log showing: total tasks processed, contacts created, contacts updated, contacts skipped, errors
• All Custom fields should be created and synced within the initial cycle
7. Loop Guard & Deduplication
• All sync flows must include a loop guard preventing infinite sync cycles
• Events originating from the automation itself must be tagged and ignored on receipt
8. Automatic Token Management
• Whatsapp API uses OAuth2 client credentials expiring every 15 days
• A scheduled n8n workflow must refresh the token automatically every 14 days
• All Whatsapp API API calls must use the stored token dynamically via n8n static data
9. Assignment Sync
• Coordinator assignment on ClickUp must mirror to Whatsapp API agent assignment
• Round-robin must be hash-based (same phone = same coordinator always on all platforms , any change in assignment should reflect on all platforms)
• Coordinator mapping between ClickUp user IDs and Whatsapp API agent IDs must be configurable
10. Communication Logging
• Every WhatsApp message or phone call must be logged as a structured comment on the ClickUp task
• Log must include: Channel, Direction, Started At, Concluded At, Duration, Outcome, Agent, Summary
• Log must simultaneously update the Whatsapp API contact's status property
________________________________________
Workflow Architecture Required
Delivered as separate importable n8n JSON files:
File Purpose
[login to view URL] All Whatsapp API inbound events → ClickUp
[login to view URL] All ClickUp changes → Hatif
[login to view URL] Google Form → ClickUp + Hatif
[login to view URL] Auto Whatsapp API token renewal
[login to view URL] One-time historical data sync
[login to view URL] ClickUp field additions/deletions → Hatif
________________________________________
Technical Requirements
• Built entirely on n8n using standard nodes (Webhook, HTTP Request, Code, Switch, IF, Schedule)
• All credentials stored as environment variables — no hardcoded secrets in JSON
• Compatible with n8n v2.20.8 self-hosted Docker
• No third-party n8n community nodes — base nodes only
• Must include setup documentation covering: all webhook URLs to register in Whatsapp API and ClickUp, all environment variables required, how to trigger the migration, how to test each flow end to end
________________________________________
API References
• ClickUp API: Available via ClickUp developer documentation
• Voxa API: Available via Voxa developer documentation (will be shared from our side upon hire)
• Auth token endpoint: POST to the Voxa OAuth2 token endpoint (OAuth2 client credentials, scope: VoxaAPI) — endpoint will be shared upon hiring
• Whatsapp API docs: Available via developer portal (password-protected , will be shared with hired freelancer)

projects.title: Vivid 2026 Food Truck Shoot
projects.full_description:
Sydney Food Truck Photographer/Videographer Needed — Vivid 2026, Circular Quay

I run Crescent Yum Cha, a halal yum cha food trailer trading during Vivid Sydney 2026 at the Overseas Passenger Terminal / Circular Quay.

I’m looking for a Sydney-based photographer/videographer or social media content creator to capture high-energy, natural content for Instagram, TikTok, YouTube Shorts, Facebook, website, and future marketing.

This is for one paid test shoot night first, with the option to book 1–2 additional nights during Vivid if the first shoot is a good fit.

This is not a slow studio shoot or a polished corporate video job. It is a fast-moving night event with crowds, food service, steam, staff, customers, lights, movement and the Vivid Sydney atmosphere.

The goal is to create a large social media content bank that we can use immediately and continue using over the next 3–6 months.

I have attached reference images/videos of the trailer, product and setup. Please note: the goal is not to make the setup look perfect exactly as-is, but to creatively capture the strongest angles — food, steam, hands, staff, customer energy, dusk light and Vivid atmosphere — while avoiding or minimising weaker lighting/signage angles where possible.

What I need captured:

The focus is on candid, energetic, street-food/documentary-style content, including:

- close-up food shots of dumplings, bao, steam, sauces, trays and serving moments
- staff preparing, steaming, packing, serving and interacting with customers
- hands, food, steam, movement, lights, crowds and atmosphere
- customers receiving food, eating and reacting where appropriate
- natural customer moments without being intrusive
- the Crescent Yum Cha trailer in action
- Vivid Sydney/Circular Quay atmosphere in the background
- vertical video snippets suitable for Reels, TikTok and YouTube Shorts

The style should feel natural, energetic, social-media-first and authentic, not overly corporate, staged or overproduced.

Important creative note:

The trailer looks best around dusk/blue hour. After dark, the top LED lighting/signage is not ideal, so I do not want the shoot to focus heavily on wide shots of the trailer signage at night.

I need someone who can creatively hide or soften the weaker parts of the setup and focus on the strongest visuals:

- food close-ups
- steam
- hands
- staff energy
- customer interaction
- movement
- crowd energy
- Vivid glow/background
- tight, flattering angles

Wide trailer shots are still useful, but mainly around dusk or when the lighting/background makes the shot look strong.

Customer filming note:

This is a public event, but I want customer filming handled respectfully. Natural customer moments are great where appropriate, but please avoid being intrusive, especially with families or anyone who may not want to be clearly filmed.

We may also be able to help create some customer-style moments with staff or willing customers if needed.

Deliverables for the first shoot night:

Please quote for approximately 2–3 hours onsite.

For the first shoot night, I am looking for:

- a large batch of raw photos
- raw vertical video clips
- 10–20 lightly edited/social-ready photos
- 1–3 short edited vertical clips, approximately 10–30 seconds each
- files delivered via Google Drive, Dropbox, WeTransfer or similar

I value quantity of usable social content as well as polished selects. I do not need every single image heavily edited. A large raw content bank is very important.

Timing:

Ideal shoot time would be approximately:

5:00pm/5:30pm to 8:00pm/8:30pm

This allows us to capture:

- dusk/blue hour
- the trailer before full darkness
- early food service
- food close-ups
- staff action
- the Vivid crowd atmosphere

Turnaround:

Because this is a live event, fast turnaround matters.

Please only apply if you can provide:

- a small batch of edited hero content within 24–48 hours
- 1 short edited reel/clip within 24–72 hours
- full raw photos/video files within 3–7 days

To apply, please include:

1. Your Sydney availability during Vivid
2. Your rate for one 2–3 hour shoot
3. Your rate for 2–3 additional shoot nights if the first night goes well
4. Examples of food, event, night, street, hospitality or social media content
5. What camera/video setup you use
6. Whether you provide RAW photo files and raw video clips
7. Your turnaround time
8. Whether you are comfortable working in busy crowds and low-light environments
9. Whether you are comfortable shooting natural social-first vertical content, not just traditional photography

This job is ideal for someone who understands social media content, food, candid event photography, fast-moving hospitality environments and night/event shooting.

Budget:

Please note: this project budget is for the first paid test shoot night only.

Budget for the first 2–3 hour shoot is AUD $450–$750, depending on portfolio, experience, deliverables and turnaround.

If the first shoot is a good fit, there is potential to book 1–2 additional shoot nights during Vivid as separate follow-up bookings.

Possible total spend across multiple nights may be AUD $1,500–$2,000+, but this first project is only for one paid test shoot night.

Please do not quote for a large corporate video production. This is a fast, social-media-first content shoot: polished selects plus a large raw content bank.

projects.title: Log Booking App UI Design
projects.full_description:
I need a clean, modern mobile-first UI built in Figma for a log booking application. The core objective is an effortlessly user-friendly booking system aimed at the general public, so every screen should feel familiar even to first-time users.

Three interface elements sit at the heart of the experience: 
• a calendar view that makes date selection intuitive, 
• straightforward search and filter controls to locate specific logs quickly, 
• and a concise, reassuring booking-confirmation page that closes the loop without friction.

Please follow current iOS and Android design guidelines, apply consistent typography and colour styles, and structure components so they can be reused easily.

Deliverables 
• A well-organised Figma file (components, styles, auto-layout) 
• Clickable prototype of the complete booking flow 
• Export-ready assets and screen annotations for hand-off 

The project is considered complete once the prototype accurately reflects the full booking journey, interacts smoothly on mobile, and all assets are neatly grouped for development.

projects.title: Increase Pop-Up Ad Clicks
projects.full_description:
I run a service-based website that reaches visitors from all over the world, and my revenue depends heavily on the pop-up ads displayed across the site. Right now the click-through rate is lower than I’d like, so I’m looking for a specialist who can lift those numbers in a compliant, sustainable way.
As soon as I open my website, a third-party ad automatically pops up. I want genuine clicks on those ads.

Here’s what success looks like to me: 
• A clear strategy to draw genuine, interested users toward each pop-up without harming the overall user experience. 
• Tactical improvements—whether that means smarter triggers, sharper creative, A/B tests, or refined targeting—that translate into a measurable uptick in clicks. 
• Detailed before-and-after reporting so I can see exactly how each change affects performance and make data-driven decisions moving forward. 

You’re free to use any mix of user-experience tweaks, analytics insights, and traffic-building methods you trust, provided they keep the site within ad-network policies and avoid artificial or incentivized clicks. If this sounds like your kind of project, let’s talk through your approach and timeline.

projects.title: WebSite Traffic
projects.full_description:
I need a specialist who can measurably increase traffic to my website with one clear objective—turning visits from technology-sector businesses into sales. Everything you plan and execute should trace back to conversions.

My target audience is strictly B2B, so tactics must resonate with decision-makers in tech companies. I am open to a mix of channels—SEO, high-intent keywords, LinkedIn or Google Ads, targeted content outreach, even niche communities—as long as they drive qualified leads and you can prove it with data.

Deliverables
• Traffic growth plan outlining channels, budgets and projected conversion impact 
• Campaign build-out (ads, keywords, landing page tweaks, tracking setup) 
• Weekly performance reports highlighting sessions, lead quality, and cost-per-conversion 
• Final wrap-up with insights and next-step recommendations

Acceptance Criteria
• Minimum 20 % lift in organic or paid sessions from tech-focused businesses within the agreed timeframe 
• Documented improvement in conversion rate and cost-per-lead compared with current baseline 
• All analytics and ad accounts shared with me in real time

Show me examples where you grew traffic for a comparable B2B tech client and outline the tools you’ll use (e.g., Google Analytics 4, Search Console, SEMrush, LinkedIn Campaign Manager). Looking forward to a concise plan that converts eyeballs into revenue.

projects.title: Delaware LLC Franchise Tax Filing -- 2

Your role is to prepare the report, apply the lowest tax calculation appropriate for an inactive entity, submit payment through the Delaware Division of Corporations portal, and return the official confirmation. I’m not certain whether an EIN is on file and I don’t remember ever submitting a report before, so I’ll rely on you to confirm those points and flag any missing years if they exist. 

When you reply, please let me know:
• Your experience handling Delaware franchise-tax or annual report filings 
• The exact information or documents you will need from me to move forward (e.g., formation certificate, signature, payment method, portal access) 
• How long the job will take once you have everything 
• Your total, all-inclusive fee 

The engagement is complete when I have the State-stamped receipt and a PDF copy of the filed report in my inbox. Fast, accurate, and penalty-free filing is the priority; future annual work is available if this goes smoothly.

We’re building a horse racing gaming platform with a Web3 layer behind it. The game itself is being developed in Unity, while the blockchain side handles things like token utility, staking, rewards, and ownership features.

This is mainly a frontend engineering role.

You’ll be working on:

- Staking dashboard and token-related UI
- Wallet connection flows and transaction states
- Responsive frontend components and layouts
- Frontend integration with backend APIs and smart contracts
- Improving UX around blockchain interactions
- Cleaning up and scaling frontend architecture as the platform grows

You’ll work closely with backend and blockchain developers, but we’re specifically looking for someone strong on the frontend side.

What We’re Looking For

- Strong React experience and solid frontend fundamentals
- Experience building production-ready dashboards or web apps
- Good understanding of component architecture, state management, async handling, and API integration
- Attention to UI/UX details and responsive design
- Experience with [login to view URL] or wallet integrations is a plus, but not required
- Someone practical, reliable, and comfortable working in a fast-moving startup environment

Why This Role

We’re still early enough that frontend decisions actually matter. You won’t just be maintaining old code — you’ll help shape how the platform feels and works as we grow.

Good fit for someone who enjoys building real products and wants to work on something more interesting than another standard SaaS dashboard.

If it sounds interesting, let’s talk.
